text: Background: Hypertension remains a leading cause of cardiovascular morbidity worldwide.
label: BACKGROUND

text: Objective: We evaluated whether a pharmacist-led intervention improves blood pressure control.
label: OBJECTIVE

text: Methods: In this randomized trial, adults were assigned to intervention or usual care for 12 weeks.
label: METHODS

text: Results: Systolic blood pressure decreased by 8.2 mmHg in the intervention group versus 2.1 mmHg in controls.
label: RESULTS

text: Conclusions: Pharmacist-led care improved blood pressure outcomes and should be considered in primary care.
label: CONCLUSIONS

text: Methods: We analyzed adverse events using intention-to-treat logistic regression.
label: METHODS

text: Results: Serious adverse events were rare and not significantly different between groups.
label: RESULTS

text: Background: Prior evidence for this intervention in community clinics is limited.
label: BACKGROUND
